Glenn 给那些无私付出的人发支票，然后谈职位和愿景，留下那些有能力和干劲的人，创业的种子才能保留。现在的估值并不代表什么，而后通过这个视频的传播，这个企业在全美可以开出无数家连锁店。这个企业的后劲很足，通过这个视频的传播，这个企业在全美可以开出无数家连锁店。
> 成功经营企业的一个要点就是找到各行业的专家。
我特意搜索下，这家 Underdog BBQ 已经有个人网站，主营业务还是当初 Glen 定下的三点：烧烤、啤酒与网上销售（服装、酱汁等）。
现在看见这张合照，感慨万千，原来如此真实，只可惜我在团队里没有看见 Matt 的介绍。
创业伊始，就用愿景和股权来招揽人才加盟。当然，不是每个人都愿意加入。招人和谈判是个持续的工作。你得找到和你价值观一致的人一起做事业，否则很容易失。
> 开一家能代表当地的店，有共鸣够真实； > > 重点在于让团队相信有共同的目标； > > 让他们愿意投入心力。
**4、情绪管理，抗压处理**
Glenn 在实践中几乎和所有团队成员都有深入交谈，团队管理特别关键，成员抗压能力预示着接下来的结局。
这里印象最深的就是 Christine。都说性格决定命运，看完她的表现和反应之后，深以为然，难以沟通，情绪暴躁，很多时候会影响人的判断。
Christine 的烧烤实力不错，也愿意学习和改进。烧烤节当天她是因为忙到团团转，没有精力管库存，可以理解当时她已经连续奋战几天，压力大，易怒。第二天还是原材料不够，造成营业额受到影响，那就没办法推卸责任，第三天两人爆发矛盾，最终和解，换人管理库存，成功解决问题。
开业前几天，Glenn 和她面谈今后的定位，并非问责，可是她先入为主认为 Glen 就是要找理由开除她，然后扔下收音设备就走了，她没有等到 Glenn 跟大家公开身份的那一刻。
最后 Glenn 开了一万美元的支票给她，但她错失了成为烤肉店股东的机会，不是因为能力不够，而是情绪失控，抗压能力不够。
> 我们每个人都能找借口放弃，然而没有人放弃，别人常常问我，你怎么有办法开大公司，你怎么敢做这些疯狂的事，我都说别给自己找借口。
切记，浓缩在纪录片中的商业经验，若非切身经历，其实并无益处。
**成功的确不是偶然。有时间抱怨，不如取长补短，培养商业思维能力，面对一切信息，比别人多想一步。**

item.title: Been There, Done That
item.description: We are social animals and our happiness comes from those who are dear to us.
item.content:encoded:
As I peeked the smiling souls on the front decorative wall on the last day of my tenure in a startup, a sense of loss and relief simultaneously slipped in my mind, without any prescience ofthe forthcoming odyssey. Pretending to be impervious to the surroundings, I somehow let myself indulge in the hit of mindfulness.
Burning with uneasiness inherent in my bones, I was taken to reflecting on the eventful chapter of my life. With much to digest, I had to readjust my objectives in my perception on startups.
**In my last exposure on self-reliance, it is a point of honor that we shall be the creators and masters of ourselves**. Last episode of working fantasies turned into a wishful thinking. My sheer disgust led to my wishful thinking, and everything started to fall apart. At the time, one of my roommates desperately invited me to participate his new startup. He had been a professional tennis athlete with resources in the tennis circle. Without thorough contemplation, I took it for granted that it was my biggest opportunity after graduation. Meticulous as I may be, the concept of startup and doing business was far beyond my understanding, and the only and right avenue to prove was to act right now.
In advent of national industrial reform, everything was so mesmerizing: venture capitals were hunting for the next promising seeds, a multitude of startups were eagerly pursuing their aspirations, every tennis players were rejoicing at the sight of huge prizes. As for us, we were convinced that we could make a difference with off-beaten path and enthusiasm.
The 2015 Tennis Carnival was a hit, at least in the eyes of the traditional media. We set up a fire, but it went down as we groped in the darkness and struggled without flashlights.
**History would tell us that unproved fantasies only bring ridicule and dreams were just beautiful pretense for daydreamers.**
---
Nothing was more audacious and meandering than our initiation. I was ready to stir a new wave of revolution upon my arrival, but the harsh truth stood in the way. My hands were tied when we were preoccupied with fixing bugs and drafting the first mockup. Four months had witnessed my disputes and conflicts with our outsourcing company.
Mr.Ge, as I later realized, was one of the three musketeers of outsourcing business in his company. Our stories went far beyond our social interaction. Day by day, I would summarize all the latest ideas and designs for developing, and in turn, he would deliver much-to-fix products; I would insist on the smooth procedure and user experience, and he would refute me with programming languages which failed to convince me. Our cooperation went astray as I expected. At times, I could not tolerate his imperfect solutions, and shout at him through the cellphone. I had to be honest, all of the ugly protests were the consequence of my inexperience.
As I fully devoted myself to crafting the best product I could possibly imagine, the rest of our team were formulating the business plan and marketing strategies. More often than not, a sense of misunderstanding and loneliness was all over me. **As for the management, products were at the core of the ecosystem. However, their ambitions turned out to another excuse for future financing.**
---
The first influential game was approaching and we were racking our brains for accommodating the playoffs in the game at our convenience. With some major functions unfinished, we had to conduct an online meeting for alternative solutions. Manual operation on databases was the best choice.
It was a Friday night, I sat on a chair at the balcony, listening to their discussions and disputes, and communicating with texts because my girlfriend had gone to bed early. Sleepiness had crept in and crushed my spirit as my watch indicated 3 a.m under the moonlight. So intense and competitive was our working schedule that we had to arrive the gymnasium at 8:00 a.m, which left me no more than three hours to sleep tight. My head accidentally hit the wall as I unconsciously fell to the ground.
Numb.
The next day I grabbed my clothes and dash to the destination. Fortunately, our efforts were rewarding as the first product showdown survived marketing trials.
**All of these personal anecdotes taught me one lesson: wrestling with outsourcing had rendered me with patience, an indispensable trait for life,** as I reflected countless exchanges with Mr.Ge.
---
Since then, the fire was spreading over Shanghai as well as some major cities. Our operation manager L led a series of victories in attracting clubs to be our major alliances in less than three months. Our creative moves and optimization of game management process became enthralling. The 2016 Tennis Carnival proved our competence in delivering the best performance and participation of the tennis circle.
**With brand awareness and recognition, product once again became the center of our discussion. **Before the Spring Festival, X officially went on board, and I had benefited from his innovative perception and professionalism. Gradually, our department was all the more significant than others, but the dilemma was particularly troublesome thanks to little or no assistance from the company.
At the end of August, it was a time for a perfect presence in the renowned and long-standing Shanghai Rolex Masters where superior tennis athletes as well as crazy fans would spare no efforts to earn the tickets. Since its commencement in September, our team had worked days and nights to orchestrate the best confrontations and dramatics in a tennis game. *The Road to Master* was appealing to every tennis participant. Frequent challenges revived the passion during 45-day qualifying contests. Only by triumphing the best players in your level could one be able to be selected in the final performance with world tennis athletes.
When I stood at the court in Qizhong Tennis Center, circumventing the stylish and magnificent environment, my heart accelerated with sincere hope that everything be all right. Luckily, our contest and a special edition of TV show deserved more attention and accolades.
To my astonishment, the climax concluded with our exhaustion and enervation.
Something I observed had finally surfaced.
**Our businesses were heating up, but our ambitions were cooling down.**
When everyone was curious about our next strike, the door suddenly slammed.
---
When the conflagration of tennis from August to October had ultimately died down, the management had declared that everyone take regular shifts to rejuvenate and stay away from the protracted battle. Our efforts were somehow compensated. However, there were clandestine financing rumors spreading around, which tickled our temptation and clouded our judgement.
One week after the crusade, we were called to attend the not-so-often-held meeting where the management highly commended our sacrifice and furthermore, hoped that everyone would return with refreshing spirit for the most crucial due diligence by several venture capitals.
At that time, I was so dumbfounded as to question the rationale and motive of their investment. **We put on a mask, and they fell on prey. **That was my logic because all the messages and strategic planning were at the mercy of the management, and I had to say, without transparent communication and effective institutions, we were like helpless lambs waiting to be salvaged.
---
Such turmoil had long been growing in my mind. The early confrontation began when our department minced no words in rebuking the management's decision in independent mobile application only for cajoling less qualified investors in early April. Every move was totally dictatorial and inflexible. To my surprise, they seemed to be quite delighted in pursuing their fantasies.
It was extremely intense and something would have exploded if the decision was pushed forward without our consent. One of the impressive remarks the management had disclosed was that since they "owned" this company, so it was imperative that proposals be executed without any dissent. From my perspective, it was absolutely nonsense and would jeopardize our cause with limited resources. In the end, my friend L, founder of this company, had gone great lengths in persuading both sides to compromise and focus on the bigger picture. **Sometimes, compromise was the better choice if you were the underdog.** Fortunately, the management continued his developing schedule and we accentuated the prospective returns.
It was the first time that I had serious doubts about the future and what I could do to reverse the tide.
---
The more critical moment came when our ideal business was based on the traditional marketing and branding essential to ensure the revenues without perfect products. Never had it crossed my mind that the management was sticking to the orthodox business rather than embracing the innovative industry. **Conventional wisdom enlightened me that the fundamental paradox between users and revenues always stood in the way.** As it dawned on me that painstaking struggle would only result in futile endeavor, I could not help asking myself what on earth I was doing and why I had been blind to all of these tricks. At the same time, other members in the company shared my deepest concerns, but some of them ignored the brutal fact and went on idling away the most precious treasure in life.
I lamented, reflected and decided to mind my own business. Without further inquiring details, I would still do my best in mundane work, which had failed to inspire me. During the Spring Festival, I would jump at the chance to openly discuss with my dad. He tried his best to empathize with my dilemma, and the honest conversations shed some light in my aimless career.
---
It was time to leave behind what I had sacrificed.
My annoucement was a surprise to all, as I had expected. However, I did not regret and exchange my heart with all of them. Deep inside, I was nostalgic and sentimental. Though everything would be gone in smoke, the experiences and failures would no doubt make me stronger and even more rational down the road.
At the end of March 31st, I handed in my laptop and took the metro back to home.
---
After several days away from work, I was urged by my ego to speak out and write down all the lessons so as to remind myself and perhaps others who encounter similar episodes.
Here is my practical advice to those who are willing to change the world:
As for the management, ideas will never suffice, and strategy and execution are the overriding definition of the highest possibility a startup could tap.
Endless discussions, frequent schedule changes, dictatorial management will crush our morale. On the contrary, companies with consistent strategies and audacious execution will be more likely to triumph in the competitive market.
As for the team, mutual growth matters.
The worst case will be that members in a team share different perspectives but will never succumb to the other's opinion. Blindness and feelings consume their promising career, and as a result, they will part ways and even become enemies in the future.
Mutual growth, an expression that Kelvin Kelly highlights in his popular book *Out of Control*, is originally intended for explaining natural dependence in biology. However, it can be also applied to social relationships because we are social animals and our happiness comes from those who are dear to us. Enterprises are encouraged to let the team be more self-reliant and cooperative.
As for the individual, lifelong learning becomes predominant in careers, especially when one is fortunate to participate the revolutionary industry in person.
When all learning is routinely accessible, there is no illusion that the world would still be the one a decade ago. Besides, the market is innovating to enable everyone to learn and earn in new ways, but it will always rest on our shoulders to carry on the torch.
Been there, done that.
As the previous chapter has come to an end, I believe my faith will always spark.

item.title: 700 Sessions Later: The Full Blueprint Behind My 6-Agent AI System
item.description: 19 cron jobs. 6 specialized agents. One Mac Mini M4. What works, what broke, and the exact files you can copy into OpenClaw today.
item.content:encoded:
19 cron jobs. 6 specialized agents. One Mac Mini M4.
In five weeks, this system went from a 209-line config disaster to producing daily content drafts, alpha research, portfolio tracking, and design — mostly while I sleep.
But it also fabricated financial reports, broke silently for 13 days, and hallucinated rules that didn't exist.
This is the full system. What works, what broke, and the exact files you can copy into OpenClaw today.
---
## The Stack
- **Hardware:** Mac Mini M4 16GB - **Orchestration:** OpenClaw - **Model:** Claude Opus 4.6 (creative/research), Claude Sonnet 4.6 (ops/analysis), GPT-5.3-Codex (Fallback), Nano Banana 2 (images) - **Subscription:** Claude Max 20x ($200), ChatGPT Plus ($20), Google One ($20) - **Channels:** Telegram (decisions) + Discord (operations, 9 channels)
## The Team
| Agent | Role | Channel | One-liner | | ----------- | ----------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | | Samantha 🧡 | Coordinator | Telegram + #general | Routes tasks, daily briefings, team memory | | Loki 🐍 | Writer | #content | Tweets, long-form, EN/CN translation | | Vision 👁️ | Researcher | #research | Daily alpha radar, project scoring, signal filing | | Jarvis ⚙️ | Coder | #coding | Tools, scripts, PRs only (never push direct) | | Shuri 🎨 | Designer | #design | Pixel art, brand visuals, taste rules compliance | | Friday 📊 | Analyst | #data | Portfolio, expenses, weekly metrics |
Each agent gets spawned per task via `sessions_spawn`. No long-running sessions. One task, one contract, one deliverable.
---
## The 3 Files That Run Everything
### File 1: AGENTS.md (75 lines)
This used to be 209 lines. Personalities, philosophy, edge cases I'd never hit. Agents got confused and followed rules meant for other agents.
Now it's a routing table. Four sections:
```markdown # AGENTS.md — Routing Directory (not a rulebook)
## Team Table | Agent | Channel | Responsibility | |-------|---------|---------------| | Samantha | Telegram/#general | Coordinator, memory, briefings | | Loki | #content | Content drafts, voice DNA, translation | | Vision | #research | Alpha radar, project research, signals | | Jarvis | #coding | Tools, scripts, PRs (never push direct) | | Shuri | #design | Images, brand visuals, taste rules | | Friday | #data | Portfolio, finance, weekly metrics |
## Workflow 1. Rex requests → Samantha routes to agent 2. Agent reads relevant rules (not all rules) 3. Agent executes with completion contract 4. Output → file + review + deliver
## Rules Routing - Spawning agents → read data/agents/rules/completion-contract.md - Content tasks → read skills/rex-ink/voice-dna/ - Design tasks → read design-system/taste-rules.md - Finance tasks → read data/agents/rules/finance-rules.md
## Non-Negotiables - Output goes to files, not just chat - Create PRs, never push direct - No fabricated data — if unsure, say so - One task, one contract, one session ```
**The principle:** agents read only the rules their current task needs. No agent reads everything. Less context = better output.
### File 2: Completion Contract (per task)
This was my biggest unlock. Agents don't know when they're done. They know when they've generated enough tokens to feel done. Different things.
Every task ships with explicit conditions:
```markdown ## Completion Contract Task is NOT complete until ALL conditions are met: 1. Draft written to output/drafts/[date]-[slug].md 2. CIS quality score ≥ 7 (self-assessed) 3. Zero placeholder content or fabricated data 4. All data points verified from real files 5. File path reported back ```
For coding: ```markdown 1. Code runs without errors 2. Output pasted in response 3. File path reported 4. PR created (not pushed to main) ```
For research: ```markdown 1. Report in output/research/[date]-[slug].md 2. Numerical score (1-10) with reasoning 3. Actionable recommendations (not just summary) 4. Signal data written to data/signals/ ```
**Not guidance. Hard boundary.** "Not meeting these conditions = task failure."
### File 3: Memory (3 layers)
``` MEMORY.md → Institutional brain (projects, team, finances) memory/sync.md → Real-time decision log (timestamped) memory/lessons.md → Mistakes by category (dated) ```
Example sync entry: ``` [2026-03-01 14:30] [samantha] Rex confirmed: fixed opus 4.6 for all creative agents ```
Example lesson: ``` [2026-02-27] FINANCE: portfolio-rebalance.py failed silently for 13 days. Wrong file path after update. No error logs. Dashboard showed stale data. Fix: All scripts must write last_run timestamp to output file. ```
Memory gets pruned on a strict schedule: P0 = permanent, P1 = 90 days, P2 = 30 days. Without pruning, context bloats and agents slow down.
---
## The 19 Cron Jobs
| Time | Job | Agent | | --------- | ----------------------- | -------- | | 01:00 | thinking-vision | Vision | | 01:30 | thinking-loki | Loki | | 02:00 | thinking-friday | Friday | | 02:30 | thinking-jarvis | Jarvis | | 03:00 | thinking-shuri | Shuri | | 03:30 | thinking-samantha | Samantha | | 04:00 | autonomous-employee | Samantha | | 07:00 | morning-delivery | Samantha | | 08:00 | alpha-radar-daily | Vision | | 09:00 | content-draft-morning | Loki | | 10:00 | daily-reading | Samantha | | 10:30 | podcast-digest | Samantha | | 17:00 | clawfeed-evening | Default | | 18:00 | content-draft-evening | Loki | | 19:30 Mon | meme-update-weekly | Loki | | 09:30 Sat | twitter-weekly-review | Friday | | 19:00 Sat | agent-ops-weekly | Loki | | 20:00 Sun | weekly-framework-review | Samantha | | 22:00 | evening-report | Samantha |
Each thinking cron (01:00-03:30) scans for strategic gaps, produces proposals or reports "no action needed." Not every run produces output — and that's by design.
---
## What Broke (Honest List)
**Silent failure (13 days).** Portfolio script broke after a path change. No errors, no alerts. Dashboard showed old data that looked normal. Caught it during a random code review. → Fix: every script writes `last_run` timestamp. Review checks freshness.
**Fabricated data.** Content agent wrote "8%+ stablecoin yields on Minswap and Spectrum." Numbers, platforms, mechanics — all made up. Looked completely real. → Fix: completion contract rule — "zero fabricated data, verify from real files first."
**Auth rotation chaos.** Sub-agent tokens expire. When they do, tasks fail silently or produce garbage. Happened twice a day during the worst week. → Fix: dedicated `google-token-refresh` cron at 05:30 daily.
**Context overflow.** AGENTS.md at 209 lines. Agents hallucinated rules, mixed responsibilities, followed instructions meant for other agents. → Fix: 209 → 75 lines. Routing table, not encyclopedia.
**Duplicate content.** Morning cron produced a "bonus draft" using the same material as yesterday's published tweet. No dedup check. → Fix: draft generation now checks last 7 days of output/ before writing.
**Image generation failures.** Missing API keys in sub-agent environment. First 3 attempts failed before discovering the key wasn't inherited. → Fix: explicit `GEMINI_API_KEY` sourced from config before generation.
---
## The Compound Effect (Five Weeks+)
This is the part nobody writes about because it takes months to experience.
**Taste extraction compounds.** Every time I edit a draft, the system diffs original vs my version and extracts a rule. 22 rules across 5 editing sessions. First drafts are measurably closer to what I'd write. The gap between draft and final shrinks with each iteration.
Example rules extracted from real edits: - #13: Parallel examples → use bullet list, not paragraphs - #18: Concrete product names > vague claims ("Claude Max 20x" not "enough tokens") - #19: Strong parallel ending needs no explanation sentence - #21: End with engagement question, not reflective lesson
**Routing gets sharper.** Early: tasks misrouted constantly. Content-adjacent research went to the writer. System issues bounced between agents. After 700 sessions, the routing table is tight. Six agents, six clearly bounded scopes.
**Contracts get specific.** First contracts: "Write a good report." Current contracts: file path, required sections, scoring methodology, verification steps. The definition of "done" evolved through hundreds of iterations.
---
## If You're Starting Today
1. One agent. One job. One completion contract with 4 conditions. 2. See what breaks. Fix with one rule. Not five. 3. Add a second agent only when the first is reliable. 4. Write rules after failures, not before. Pre-written rules are guesses. 5. Review weekly. Kill rules that aren't working. Merge overlaps. 6. Keep memory lean. Expire what's stale on a strict schedule.
**My single realization after 700 sessions:**
The agents are fine. The engineering around them is what matters. Your config is a routing table. Your rules are hypotheses. Your contracts are the only thing between "attempted" and "completed."
Tend the system weekly. Pull the weeds. That's the whole secret.

item.title: 聊聊《细节》的那些事
item.description: 读《细节：如何轻松影响他人》有感——关于职场沟通、个人激励、错误反思和环境暗示的思考。
item.content:encoded:
第一次了解到《细节·如何轻松影响他人》这本书，还是通过罗辑思维推荐的。那天的语音介绍了一本被戏称为"教人们学套路"的书，当下看到的一切都令我十分兴奋，我清晰地记得几个重要的小技巧，如何有效沟通、如何自我激励等。
碰巧自己也是处于创业阶段，项目进展与团队沟通上都无人引领，自顾自地摸爬滚打，遍体鳞伤都浑然不觉。当时，公司正忙于与电视台合作的大型赛事活动，项目负担十分繁重，不觉压力倍增。总认为，自己的项目管理能力还算不错，可真正要应付大型项目的各项沟通稍显稚嫩，而更为要命的是，我对于细节的把握总是欠火候，需求反复修改、上线日期被迫耽搁。
事实证明，订购本书绝对是正确选择。这本书由《影响力》作者罗伯特·西奥迪尼与另两位知名的说服力与影响力研究的权威合作完成。在影响力和说服力领域，罗伯特·西奥迪尼的著作《影响力》已被翻译成 26 种语言，在全球售出了 200 万册，并被《财富》杂志评选的 75 本必读的最睿智的图书之一。
纵观全书，我很喜欢本书的核心观点：
> 当你想要创造出惊人影响力的时候，细节处最微小的做法改变，往往能够取得最显著的成效。
全书中，三位作者共提供了 52 条细节建议，这些细节覆盖了职场沟通、员工激励、商业谈判、家庭教育等诸多方面，很多看似不起眼的细节却能够产生天差地别的影响。
我一直相信，人生的精彩程度取决于个人对于细节的重视程度。或许乍看之下，本书推崇细节是成功的决定因素，而作者想要表达的是，在同等条件下，运用一些被科学研究证实过的"四两拨千斤"的办法更能打动对方、实现目标。
---
## 关于职场沟通
职场是人生成长的不可或缺部分，大多数人都要面对社会，从懵懂少年变成职场老鸟，成长的速度却因人而异。
我自己便是如此，之前有段时间心情一直低落，我作为产品经理负责监工，却对项目毫无参与感。我十分认可大家认真负责的态度，碌碌无为并非本愿，究其原因在于自己专业能力不够，更缺乏有效沟通。产品经理是公司的立交桥，销售、交互、开发、运营都要考虑与及时沟通项目进展。
在《细节》中，作者有两点建议：
**第一，认真表达感激。** 向提供帮助的人明确表达感激之情，率先给予是格外好用的工具。在产品上线前，向公司全员发送邮件，通告项目进展，同时明确感激提供帮助的同事，这不但是职场素养，也是个人素质的体现。
**第二，如何更高效地开会。** 一是请参加会议的人在会议前提交信息，二是组织会议的人最后一个发言，三是做个任务清单，四是对会议座次安排进行微调，引导注意力重点。
---
## 关于个人激励
我很赞同其中关于"小数字假说"的细节，这一心理暗示适用于目标设定与项目执行。
> 无论你的目标是提高客户忠诚度还是激励他人积极完成任务，应在早期的时候，把目标对象的注意力引导到已经取得的少量进展上，而不是盯着尚未完成的较大数字。
此外，几乎每份工作或是技能都有自己的意义和重要性。李笑来老师也说过：无论做什么事情，在开始前，都要想尽一切办法为这个事情赋予重大的意义，甚至多重重大的意义。凡事想明白为什么，你才会有深刻认识与完美执行。
---
## 关于错误反思
本书建议对于个人工作犯错或是团队决策失误，一定要做一份属于自己的"错误清单"，将自己和他人的犯错记录下来，而一旦碰到要做重要决定之时，就去过一遍。记录错误的价值并且反思的价值是不言而喻的。
当然，我们还要摆正心态，"零错误"不如"及时改正错误"效果好，犯错在所难免，而对错误的有效行动才是获得更大影响力的关键。
---
## 关于环境暗示
俗话说，近朱者赤，近墨者黑。无论是公共事务，还是个人成长，想尽一切办法去寻找拥有那项技能的人以及人群，如果没办法一对一交流，起码也要时刻关注他们。
---
总而言之，我们对待事物的态度要关注细节，更是思维提升，凡事不能局限于常理之中，进阶思考方能让每件事情做得更加出色。
希望你能有所启发。

item.title: 其实这个世界是有生命的
item.description: 读罢「解忧杂货店」，不甚感慨：万物轮回，天道有常。
item.content:encoded:
读罢「解忧杂货店」，不甚感慨：万物轮回，天道有常。
**1**
说起讲故事的能力，东野先生要算是一流的了，迄今为止看完的小说，从「放学后」、「秘密」、「侦探伽利略」、「白夜行」、「虚无的十字架」亦或是「怪笑小说」，他都是以异乎常人的洞察力与叙事风格而被大众津津乐道。虽说大多数是悬疑小说，我更欣赏于他对人性的剖析与理解。
聊回「解忧杂货店」，看着看着，竟然不忍心翻到最后，因为故事终究会结束，而我们自己的人生还在继续。看罢小说，趁着思绪未停，匆匆写下了下面的思考（*有剧透，请谨慎决定*）。当然，我相信，或许看完了再来讨论，你会更有收获吧。
**2**
首先，真心佩服作者的时间线与逻辑脉络，能将一个聚焦的剧情通过多个人物视角逐步推进，环环相扣，统一连贯，由浪失与晓子当年感人的故事，进而演变为丸光园与解忧杂货店；一方为孤独流浪的孩子提供安身之处，一方为内心无助的他们提供精神寄托，各自凭借自己的努力守护这方水土，真的令人动容。几十年过去，他们一如既往的珍惜与保护为太多的孩子寻求正确的人生方向，善莫大焉。从现实出发的三个男生，到第一章的月兔，再到最后一章的晴美，最终以浪失的亲笔信结束，终成闭环，为小说划上了圆满的句号。
**3**
其次，透过书信与文字，我们总是能够瞥见每个人的真情实感，文字的力量不言而喻，仅仅是简单地叙事都能将读者带入情境之中，安静地展现了每个人的喜怒哀乐。如此多次的书信往来，真实记录了几个时代的变迁，人生与时代密不可分，你的每一个举动都是历史的亲历。
这让我不禁联想到最近很火的一档节目「见字如面」：**一百多封信件呈现出对应复杂历史的丰富的多样性。** 有求爱信也有分手信，有战争中敌对双方的平静通信，也有父子间唇枪舌剑的激越交锋，有名人不为人知的心事，也有荒唐年代的锥心往事。无论是情感状态、价值观念还是文字风格，都是多元呈现。
印象最深的便是黄永玉与曹禺的往来信件。信中，两位大师赤诚相见、披肝沥胆的交流，令听者无不热血沸腾，又陷入深思。（*若你有兴趣，不妨找来看看~*）
> 黄永玉：你多么需要他那点草莽精神。
> 曹禺：但愿迷途未远，还能追回已逝的时光。
历史的不断重复，人性的善恶交织，让信中的生活跟今天的每个人都息息相关。所有的历史因此也都成了当代史，成了人们身边的历史。
最后，我愈发认为，**这个世界是有生命的，不是冷冰冰的，你如何善待世界，这个世界如何善待你。** 与其纠结是否发生如此神奇的事情，不如窥探内心，你希望生活的世界究竟如何？
其实，这个自证预言的概念是由美国社会学家罗伯特·默顿（Robert K. Merton）提出的一种社会心理学现象：**一个对情境的虚假的定义引起了一种新的行为，而这种行为让最初虚假的猜想成真了。**
万事皆有因，万事皆有缘。小说中的所有人物，无不拥有自己的烦恼，爱情、亲情、友情交织其中，而最终的结局也许是最好的结局了罢。
对此，浪失熊治（解忧杂货店的主人）已经深刻地解答了：
> 我的回答之所以发挥了作用，原因不是别的，是因为他们自己很努力。如果自己不想积极认真地生活，不管得到什么样的回答都没用。
而对于一直在前进却迷茫的人而言，在小说的最后，他也给出了自己的答案：
> 地图是一张白纸，这当然很伤脑筋。任何人都会不知所措。 > > 可是换个角度来看，正因为是一张白纸，才可以随心所欲地描绘地图。一切全在你自己。对你来说，一切都是自由的，在你面前是无限的可能。这可是很棒的事啊。我衷心祈祷你可以相信自己，无悔地燃烧自己的人生。我以后应该不会再回答烦恼咨询了。感谢你在最后问了一个很有价值的难题。
希望你能够有所启发。

item.title: 感谢中本聪与比特币
item.description: 历史长河，比特币确为共识经济时代的最佳代表。这是最好的时代，也是最坏的时代。
item.content:encoded:
入圈 7 年，有幸跟随比特币的顶级叙事成长。
「这是最好的时代，也是最坏的时代」
在 $BTC 将创新高之际，这篇文章简要回顾今年 $BTC 操作与教训。
对于投资，持有不动是最简单的也最困难的策略 👇
## 第一阶段
2023 年 11 月，BTC 拉升到 4 万，经历了漫长熊市折磨，我开始纠结。
### 市场消息
- 比特币 ETF 将是史诗级利好 - 美联储放缓加息步伐 - 俄乌战争缓和
### 思考复盘
- 4 - 6 万区间震荡风险加大，盈亏比较低，有众多优秀山寨币可选择，当时 ADA 我有更多赚币的机会； - 参考火山哥判断，4 万以上先逐步撤出，等待 BTC 创新高后再介入，持仓从上轮顶点到现在一枚未动，1 月后全部卖出 3 BTC 换成 12 万 USDT
## 第二阶段
临近 ETF 正式上线，市场波动加剧，正式推出后，2 月初 ETF 开始带领 $BTC 暴涨。
冲破历史高点后，比特币随后长达 8 个多月的横盘震荡，山寨疲软，10 月底再回到 7 万以上。
思考复盘：
心里没货，有些慌乱，逐步在 6 万左右买回，同时尝试其他市场热门叙事，3 月在 $BOME 撞到好运，4 月后全面冲入比特币符文市场 与 $DOG，期间比特币减半、地域冲突等依然没有打乱节奏，我重新调整仓位，把更多利润换到 $BTC 低点定投。
只可惜，按照 $BTC 币本位，如今的资产已经失血严重，这轮牛市与以往周期表现可能不同，还需要等待时间见证。
## 经验教训
### 是否持仓，心态天差地别
盈亏同源在哪里都适用，卖完了 $BTC 才发现自己有些恍惚，好不容易买回来，还是不够坚定。
### 对于投资，专注少量优秀资产
3/4 月份我尝试了很多币圈不同赛道的赚钱方式，几乎都铩羽而归，反而对于真正长期看好的资产缺乏关注，或者仓位管理失常。
$SNEK 也是如此，只有经历过长期视角，体验过高山低谷，才能心如止水，重新上路。
### 市场噪音太多，自己做决定
比特币生态、RWA、再质押等等热门叙事，散户一不小心，就会抛掉手中筹码，加入新的罗网。
首先得明白自己是怎么样的人，适合交易还是投资。
- 从交易出发，卖出 $BTC 似乎还算正确，即便中间曲折。
- 从投资出发，卖出 $BTC 是非常糟糕的决定，从熊转牛，把最稳定有价值的资产先卖出，以至于后续影响操作。
现在已经是牛市中期，接下来的几个月绝对是这轮周期最重要的时段，希望你有所期待，保住胜利果实。
顺势而为，保持信仰，守正出奇 💪

item.title: 我们都有光明的未来
item.description: 我多希望，今天只是愚人节。因为，我们还是分道扬镳了。
item.content:encoded:
我多希望，今天只是愚人节。因为，我们还是分道扬镳了。
**1**
2013 年秋初，我来到上海读研，开学第一天我拎着行李，横穿大上海，来到了这个梦寐以求的校园。
从报到再到宿舍，一切都很顺利，未来前我便听说宁远楼条件最为优越，还被冠以鸳鸯楼的美称，男生女生分住南北。一进门，宿管阿姨就开始贴心地提醒各种生活事项，这里就是我以后两年的家了。
早在录取通知书上，我就得知自己的房间号与位置，其余三位室友的名字也赫然在目，我突然瞥见有人和我同姓，也是两个字，不由地多了几分期待。
一楼 128，这个数字现在想起来都很清晰。我先到了宿舍，四个铺位分列两端，一个人霸占一个位置，为的便是看书、学习、休息有条不紊。
> 这宿舍环境还不错诶，就是不通风，光线太暗...
我紧接着开始收拾。正当我大展身手之时，一个精气十足、戴着眼睛的男生提着行李打开了房门，他身高与我不相上下，不过比我壮实得多，看得出来是经常运动和健身的人。见我忙活着，他立马放下行李，我们互相打招呼，便聊起了家常。
> 原来我们是本家，你可以叫我 Levax，以后请多指教。
> 哈哈，我向你学习才是。我们正好分在对面，看来缘分不浅。
**人真的很奇怪，以前总是习以为常的场景，现在要认真回忆太难。**
他给我的第一感觉就是善于言谈，无论何种场合都能谈笑风生。认识他的几年间，每次一起聚餐或是活动，他都是轻而易举地成为焦点，这个气场和实力总是让我羡慕不已。
而我则是另一个世界的人：习惯了独自对话，要说交心的朋友那也屈指可数，平常不爱出风头，喜欢默默地听着别人说话，轮到自己了再简单说两句，总是点到为止。
不同的地方还有太多，虽然是室友，可我学的是国际经济与贸易，他学的是体育经营与管理，平时我只好一个人上课，下课了大家才在宿舍聊得热火朝天。
等他收拾完毕，我们就跑去旁边的学生中心办理其他手续了。至于另外两位 Henry 和 Mark，他们的故事这次就按下不表。
第一次的见面，回想起来并不特别，可这也是我们相识的第一步。
写下这段话的时候，我突然想起以前有个问题特别火，那就是回忆你和朋友第一次认识或是见面的场合，想想这才是触动人们心灵深处的故事。
不过，这份关系看上去并不牢靠，难怪熊太行说：
> 和像你的人交往，是你的心理舒适区，等到你主动去结交不像自己的人，你的境界就上了一个台阶。
**2**
读研的那两年过得太快，他是一个很有意思的人，可总是陷入爱情的龙卷风，每次听闻我都默默不语，经历多了其实也没错，每个人都有自己的选择不是，可是我总是隐约担心，他真的还要这样下去么？这或许是我们的另一个反差。
每到周五下课，我都会回到女朋友那里陪她，他却更多地在校园里寻找乐趣，或是纵情山水，或是遁入学习。
两年间，我们之间的交流其实不算多，除了体会开黑的畅快，想要真正了解一个人，我似乎并没有做多少，而我们间的插科打诨只有我们最清楚。
他总是能够完成很多我想象不到的任务，毕业求职时，他的战绩要比我好得多，最终决定到上海银行工作，毕竟不辱财大的名声。
而我就差远了，跌跌撞撞找了一份咨询公司的项目经理，而后的不愉快也由此而发。
毕业之际，我们聊起了未来的打算，我想到自己错过的那些，心里不免遗憾，纵使我努力去改变，可是临别才是最为难受的。
我送了他们各自一份礼物作为毕业赠礼，作为室友，我算不上出色。他那时热衷摄影，我特地选了摄影作品集，只可惜，这些年我很少见他用过。
至于其他两位，Henry 开启了自己的创业之旅，Mark 则去了知名的私募公司。
**我真的希望，我们都有光明的未来。**
**3**
原以为故事就要就此结束。
毕业后，Henry 不甘寂寞，希望我和 Levax 能够帮忙一起创业，我们隔两周便去他那租的房间里召开秘密会议，每个人都是全然潇洒。那时他刚起步，我们也不确定未来如何。那些日子，我们大致聊起了近期规划，从网球培训和赛事入手，当然是好事，只是时机并未成熟。
2015 年国庆回来，那时的我郁郁不得志，终于熬完了一个月，在机缘巧合之下，我决心投入互联网行业开启了新的事业，于我而言，创业机会再好不过。
可令我佩服的是，Levax 竟然也辞去了上海银行的工作，决心一起加入创业。那时我真的不理解他的决定，这是有多么大的勇气才会如此。
他随即回了我一句：**人生最珍贵的是和朋友一起实现一件事情的经历，以后回忆起来别提多美了。**
我由衷感慨，要做成一件事情太难，好在一路有人相伴。
而后的一年半，**我们经历从零到一的产品设计、市场营销、赛事策划等诸多项目，在新投资人的协助下，也算是树立了自己的品牌。** 信息化的赛事分级体系实属原创，毕竟在这垂直细分领域，要完美地服务于网球爱好者、俱乐部、赛事平台等群体难度真的不小。
**4**
**创业的确九死一生，可更令我感动的是他们曾经不顾一切地让我们留在上海。**
我和我女朋友在 2016 年回来之后，关于是否留在上海争论了太多次，以前我一直认为，**她创业是必然的，我选择了她，同样选择接受她的一切，决定了她是人生中最重要的人，决定了我会为她妥协。**
人生的重大选择无外乎就这么些，我本来已经放弃抵抗，即便我还是心有不甘。
那晚，我喊了他们两个一起吃饭，匆匆点完了麻辣香锅。我试图表现得很镇定，调整几次呼吸，遗憾地告诉他们可能要走了。不出所料，他们非常吃惊，我至今记得那时他们的表情。三个人只是愣在那里，我都能听见他们的心跳声，那种砰砰砰的声音真的让人无所适从，最后还是他打破了僵局。一晚上聊了很多，可我们却在各自沉浸自己的世界里。
此后的一周，他们在为我们筹划了太多，特意安排了聚餐，Mark 也来了，而我却全然不知。那天我们坐在火锅旁畅谈人生，Levax 换了太多方式来了解我们的想法，试图一一击破。我明白他们的良苦用心，她的态度竟然也逐渐改变了，有些话或许其他人来说更有说服力吧，即便这些话都是我一直强调的。
他们那么有耐心、那么为我们考虑，我在一旁也感受他们的真诚与期待，她真的有些听进去了。
那天过后，我们拼命想要展现工作，拼命想要留住这些美好，拼命想要证明在这个地方，一切皆有可能。
**而故事真的就愈发神奇了，我们的事业再接再厉，而她则在上海寻找到了自己的一方天地。**
**5**
然而，好景不长，去年他开始有些困惑了，可能是因为父母，可能是因为责任，他说了好几次，可能不会再待在一起奋斗了，毕竟他有自己的人生和责任。那时的契机便是一起加入了新公司，重新开始一番事业，几经考虑后，他也愿意再拼一把。
**转眼一年有余，正当公司发展蒸蒸日上之际，他却选择了功成身退。**
两周前，他无意间说到这个决定，此刻换做我吃了一惊，但冷静下来我也能够理解他的决定。
> 只是，我真的不希望被告知，既然是朋友，若是能够共同面对，何必藏着掖着，等到最后一刻等来的却是宣判？我原以为我们可以好好聊聊，只是这次，你已经做出了你的选择。
创业的这两年，他真的变了很多。
**我体会了他的不安与焦虑，他怕自己在新的职位上有所懈怠，他怕无法引领整个团队实现目标；**
**我见证了他的奋起与执念，他开始马不停蹄的学习各种知识，他开始将每天的时间安排得井井有条；**
**我也看见了他的蜕变与成长，他学会了如何管理与提升团队效率，他学会了市场运营本质上都是为了创造持续价值。**
年会上，我们两个举杯，我掏心掏肺地和他说了声，谢谢。
陈年往事何须再提，策马扬鞭方为快事。
只是，他还是提出了辞职，他说快到而立之年不敢再拼了，他说他怕落后市场，他说这是他自己作为男人的责任。
换做是我，恐怕也是一样的决定。可能我真的也没有完全了解过他，我只能真心祝福他越来越好。
敲下这些文字时，我不禁想到了太多事。**这段时间我已经感受过太多的无力感，能够坦然面对变化，这也许是另一个人生境界吧。** 絮絮叨叨想说的也就这么多，我一直想，这些感受要是不记下来，只能留在我们的回忆里了。
**我也不准备说再见，因为今年最大的改变就是学会如何开始与告别。**
Levax，感谢有你，祝好。
希望你能够有所启发。

item.title: Clarity Over Alignment: The Cost of Moral Purity
item.description: I used to cut ties with anyone whose values didn't perfectly align with mine. Then I missed three major opportunities because I was waiting for 'pure' alignment that doesn't exist.
item.content:encoded:
## The Trap of Perfect Alignment
I used to cut ties with anyone whose values didn't perfectly align with mine.
It felt righteous. Clean. Like I was protecting my integrity by surrounding myself only with people who thought exactly like me.
Then I missed three major opportunities because I was waiting for "pure" alignment that doesn't exist.
Here's the uncomfortable truth: **The world doesn't reward your moral purity. It rewards your clarity of purpose.**
---
## The Messy Reality
Claude's CEO may have blind spots.
OpenClaw's creator may hate crypto.
Your efficient friend may have messy ethics.
Your talented colleague may vote differently than you.
The mentor who taught you everything might hold views you find repulsive.
This is the real world. Everyone is a bundle of contradictions. Everyone has blind spots. Everyone is wrong about something.
The question isn't whether people align perfectly with your values. The question is: **What do you actually need right now?**
---
## Two Choices
You have two choices in this world:
**Option A:** Spend years searching for perfect alignment — and watch others build while you judge.
**Option B:** Get brutally honest about what you actually need right now, use the best tool for that specific job, and keep moving.
I spent years in Option A. It felt good. It felt safe. I had clean hands and a clear conscience.
But I also had empty hands.
While I was busy vetting everyone's moral purity, others were busy building. They weren't asking if their tools agreed with them on every issue. They were asking: "Does this work?"
---
## What I Learned the Hard Way
**The winners everywhere aren't the ones with the cleanest friend lists.**
They're the ones who know exactly what they're optimizing for.
They can work with people they disagree with because they know the goal isn't agreement — it's outcome.
They can use tools built by people who think differently because they know the goal isn't alignment — it's execution.
They can hold their own values while borrowing strength from those who see the world differently.
---
## The Framework I Use Now
I stopped asking: "Do I agree with this person on everything?"
I started asking:
- What do I need to accomplish right now? - Who has the skills/tools/connections to help me do that? - Can we work together on this specific thing despite our differences? - What's the cost of not working with them?
Sometimes the answer is still no. Some lines shouldn't be crossed. Some partnerships aren't worth the trade-off.
But most of the time, the answer is: **Let's focus on what we're building, not what we believe.**
---
## The Real Flex
The real flex isn't having perfect alignment with everyone in your circle.
The real flex is having **clarity**.
Knowing what you're building. Knowing what you need. Knowing what you're willing to trade and what you're not.
And then being flexible enough to work with imperfect people to get there.
Because here's the thing: You're imperfect too. So am I. We all have blind spots. We all hold contradictory beliefs. We all fall short of our own standards sometimes.
The question isn't whether the people around you are perfect. The question is whether you can build something meaningful with imperfect people in an imperfect world.
The answer, it turns out, is yes — if you're clear about what you're optimizing for.
---
*Stay Invested in the Game.*

item.title: The Best Birthday Gift I Ever Gave Myself
item.description: Building this website wasn't just about creating a digital presence—it was about reclaiming ownership of my story. I uploaded my first public article from 2009 and other milestone pieces, creating annotations for my reimagined life. These weren't just old posts; they were chapters of who I've become.
item.content:encoded:
## Introduction
What's the most meaningful gift you could give yourself?
For me, it wasn't something I could buy in a store or unwrap from a box. It was something I had been putting off for years—creating a digital home for all my scattered writings and thoughts from the past decade and a half.
Since my freshman year in college, I've been writing. But my content has been fragmented across various platforms, disappearing into the digital void. After stumbling upon a website-building guide, I had an epiphany: with AI advancing so rapidly, why couldn't I finally consolidate everything into one place I truly own?
This is the story of how I built my personal website in three days, the obstacles I encountered, and what I learned about "vibe coding" with AI.
## The Journey: From Idea to Reality
### Step 1: Planning with Purpose (The MRD/PRD Phase)
I started by having deep conversations with Gemini, transforming my vague idea into concrete documents—something akin to Market Requirements Documents (MRD) and Product Requirements Documents (PRD).
**But here's where I hit my first major roadblock.**
Despite my years as a product manager, I lacked deep knowledge of technical architecture and specialized platforms. I relied heavily on AI for answers, and initially, I took a wrong turn. Gemini suggested an "Astro + TinaCMS" combination that sent me down a rabbit hole.
I spent an entire weekend traveling and wrestling with TinaCMS, unable to break through. It wasn't until I re-fed the key points back to Gemini that I discovered the simpler, more elegant solution:
- **Astro** - Static site generator - **GitHub** - Code hosting - **GitHub Actions** - Automated builds - **GitHub Pages** - Website hosting - **Cloudflare** - CDN and domain management
**Lesson learned**: When working with AI, if you're stuck, don't be afraid to restart the conversation with clearer context.
### Step 2: Building the Foundation
Once I understood the correct architecture, I moved methodically:
1. Installed the standard Astro template 2. Connected to GitHub 3. Wrote and pushed my first article 4. Secured my personal domain—rexliu.io (at $45/year for long-term display) 5. Bound the domain to GitHub Pages
Monday afternoon, approximately two hours later, everything was connected. When I saw my first article live on my own domain, the joy was indescribable.
### Step 3: Designing the User Experience
Tuesday brought a new challenge: redesigning the frontend.
I gave Gemini my final directive: "Rex Liu's Minimalist Life Essays Collection" was entering Version 2.0.
I panicked initially, thinking I'd have to start over with a completely new design. But Gemini offered a solution that saved me: instead of rebuilding from scratch, I could simply swap out the content engine to Astro Cactus—a minimalist theme that perfectly matched my vision.
**Another crucial discovery**: Version control is your safety net. Whenever the frontend threw errors, I could simply roll back. This feature provided immense peace of mind. Imagine losing everything at the finish line—the frustration would have been unbearable.
By afternoon, I had resolved most issues and successfully pushed everything to my live website. That moment of accomplishment? Pure bliss.
## My Core Requirements (And How I Met Them)
✅ **Content consolidation** - All my writings now have a permanent home
✅ **Psychological satisfaction** - The pride of building something myself
✅ **Data and code ownership** - Complete control over my digital presence
✅ **Alignment with my rebirth journey** - A fresh start with deep roots
## The Complete Vibe Coding Philosophy
Through this three-day journey—the first two days stumbling, the third accelerating—I developed a framework for successful AI-assisted coding:
### 1. **Plan Your Architecture Before Starting** Think through your implementation and confirm the architecture before diving in. Avoid repetitive, wasted effort. The time you spend planning will save you double the time debugging.
### 2. **Save Important Documents Constantly** Long conversations with AI can lead to "hallucinations" or context drift. Both Claude and Gemini helped me tremendously here, but I learned to save critical information externally.
### 3. **Use Cursor for Local Editing** Copy and paste code directly rather than letting the AI auto-generate within the chat. This gives you more control and prevents unexpected "creative liberties."
### 4. **Embrace Incremental Progress** Learn to roll back and save frequently. Add one feature at a time. Don't try to accomplish everything in one heroic push.
### 5. **Done Is Better Than Perfect** This principle carried me through. I awakened memories that had been sleeping for years. I remembered how I once needed programmer friends for every little feature. Now, I can solve problems myself.
## Your Turn: What Will You Build?
Building this website wasn't just about creating a digital presence—it was about reclaiming ownership of my story.
I uploaded my first public article from 2009 and other milestone pieces, creating annotations for my reimagined life. These weren't just old posts; they were chapters of who I've become.
**Here's my challenge to you**: What have you been putting off because it seemed too technical, too complex, or too time-consuming? What would having your own digital space mean for you?
The barriers that once seemed insurmountable—coding knowledge, technical expertise, expensive developers—have dramatically lowered. AI tools don't replace human creativity; they amplify it.
## Final Thoughts
The best birthday gift I gave myself wasn't the website itself. It was the confidence that comes from building something with my own hands (and a little AI assistance).
It was the satisfaction of knowing that my words, my thoughts, my journey now have a home that I control—not at the mercy of platform algorithms or corporate decisions.
It was the realization that in 2024, anyone with determination and curiosity can bring their digital visions to life.
**What will your gift to yourself be?**
Start with one small step. Have a conversation with an AI assistant. Sketch out your vision. And remember: completion beats perfection every single time.
---
*Ready to start your own digital journey? The tools are waiting. The only question is: are you?*

item.title: 区块链时代，没有你想象中的遥远
item.description: 这是 Cardano 中文社区线下活动的一次复盘。
item.content:encoded:
一个月前，Cardano 中文翻译组的小伙伴们就开始讨论中国社区的线下见面会了，当时加入不久，心里总是跃跃欲试，想要尽力为项目做些贡献，随即就开始了中国社区的第一次见面会策划。
大家总说，Cardano 为什么不来中国？作为第三代区块链技术的领导者之一，它似乎总是和我们有种天然的距离，我们很难从公开资料中寻找到蛛丝马迹，这也难免会令人生疑。事实上，Cardano 与中国早有联系，从众筹阶段就有一部分早期投资者了解到这个项目，同时也尝试与中国高校合作，此外，从目前的战略方向上看，Cardano 的重心放在非洲。纵然如此，Cardano 爱好者们同样做了各种各样的努力来为项目注入价值。中国有如此庞大的区块链群体，必将成为下个阶段的方向，或许等到雪莱项目正式上线，权益挖矿大幕开启时，我们将会看见不一样的营销与宣传。
这就是我们开展上海见面会的背景，人们苦于被 Cardano 高冷的学术范打败，苦于缺少及时透明的中文信息，苦于长期陷入孤独的社交场合，这次的见面会无外乎就是让大家相互认识并且提升共识的过程。即便困难重重，我们也因难而上。
---
下定决心，我们便开始了自己的准备工作。组织小组由浩哥负责，他是一名资深的技术工作者，明川则是 Cardano 的忠实爱好者，对共识算法颇为心得，我则更偏重于活动宣传与现场组织工作。这一个月的工作却让我记忆犹新。 我简单地梳理了这次见面会的流程：
- 确认主题——如何让见面会主题更加吸引人？ - 定义目标——希望达到什么目标以及衡量成功的核心指标？ - 赞助、嘉宾与场地——如何让见面会更有价值？ - 物料准备——海报与现场礼品准备？ - 报名宣传——报名渠道、流程与宣传方式？ - 现场分享——项目介绍 + 主题演讲 + 爱好者交流 - 活动复盘——如何总结经验，让未来更有方法？
第一次大规模的集体见面会，其实我们心里还是没底，毕竟论经验与活动策划，我们都不是专业的。作为一个产品人，首先考虑的就是用户、场景与需求。 其实，大家参与线下见面会无非就以下几个目的： 一是大咖介绍，分享见解，答疑解惑：这一方面有嘉宾最好；二是项目进度与细节：展示其他非公开或者我们私下了解到的信息；三是项目参与：我们如何参与这个项目来，这一方面更多是投资、权益池和其他帮助在中国区推广方式四是爱好者互动：我们可以提前收集关于项目发展或是区块链技术的讨论话题，然后大家交流感想，有一定奖励更好了。
---
按照这个思路，我们最好能够邀请到项目方 IOHK、基金会或是 Emurgo 的嘉宾到场。作为官方支持的线下见面会，这一点尤为重要。主题当然就是第三代区块链技术为核心，这是 Charles 在各种场合不断提及的概念，相信对于现场参与者而言十分有吸引力。
上面的四个维度我们都有所涉及，官方更为看重这次见面会的宣传数据以及参与程度。 我们综合考虑后决定, 报名人数 150 人，到场人数 90 人 +活动推广渠道 5 个以上，各大渠道曝光量超过 10,000 次现场直播观看人数超过 500 人若是能够在第一次活动中达到以上目标，那也算完美了。
赞助方面，和基金会沟通后，他们愿意大力支持，只是限于时间紧张，这次提供的礼品未能到场，我们只能另谋出路，在活动曝光后，我们很幸运地接触了孺子牛区块链，了解到陈杰先生与 Cardano 的故事，他们愿意赞助。
嘉宾方面，我们原本敲定的人选是 Emurgo 的 Darren Camas，很可惜，活动前三天，他邮件告知我们因故无法到场，几经周折，我们邀请了陈杰先生作为开场分享者。这一方面的变化的确让我们始料未及，因为那是我们已经完成了后续的宣传工作。
场地方面，我和明川实地调研了复旦校友俱乐部，整个氛围相当完美，场地同样符合 Cardano 的学术气息。
这一方面琐事就太多了，包括海报、易拉宝、横幅、现场礼品、茶歇零食等，由浩哥全权把关，他对于项目管理与活动策划有大将风度，我们私下沟通了多次，每次都会由会议记录与相应的行动安排，这让我特别佩服。
在讨论了多次活动流程后，我们确定了活动宣传文稿。
---
我们将活动行作为宣传阵地，很可惜，他们却始终设为私密，活动曝光完全由我们负责，这让我很恼火，期间我找过他们多次，却始终没有回应。幸运的是，在各大微信群与论坛上，我们也有很多自发宣传的爱好者，这让我们的计划得以顺利落地。
其实，我想了很多种报名可能性，还是愿意将个人微信贴在活动背后，最终由我一对一沟通。十多天时间，每天都在和参与者沟通，这倒让我非常惊讶，我一直以为自己不适合做用户运营的工作，可是实地聊下来，你会发现每个人的不同。这就像我们在看他人文章时的感觉。
周六上午我们早早地来到了现场，看见那些纪念品，自己也忍不住要宣传起来。这可是真正的 ADA 纪念币，这个创意实在太酷了。
现场环节，不得不夸赞我们的另一位组织者 JS，他的英语主持震撼全场，毕竟是国际项目嘛。
见面会上首先播放了此前卡尔达诺开发团队负责人 Charles Hoskinson 与基金会中国区负责人郝雷先生在官方微博发布的对中国爱好者的问候视频。郝雷先生还专为此次卡尔达诺爱好者见面会录制了问候视频，首先表示对组织者、现场嘉宾与参与者的感谢，而后详细为大家阐述了基金会的职责与工作。
陈杰先生作为卡尔达诺项目的早起参与者，他为大家深入浅出地介绍了卡尔达诺及艾达币的前世今生，同时风趣地介绍了当初参与项目众筹的往事，以及他对于项目未来前景的看法。
>卡尔达诺是一位务实的梦想家，从老人那里学习，在社区中是一个好公民，并且总能找到支付账单的方式。我们无法预知未来，但我们很乐意努力为每个人做出更好的选择。
浩哥还是一如既往地稳重，他分享的主题围绕第三代区块链，分别从区块链演进、卡尔达诺介绍与路线图三个部分阐述他对于卡尔达诺的理解。
从第一代的比特币系统，到第二代的以太坊与智能合约，再到第三代的区块链技术，我们逐步看见区块链技术的不断演进与迭代。
第三位特邀嘉宾来自 MakerDao 中国区负责人潘超先生。他为大家带来的主题演讲是《稳定货币的缘由、模式、误区与未来》。
> 稳定货币最大的功能就是主权货币购买力在区块链资产交易的映射，充当记账单位、交易媒介和价值储藏的功能。他非常看好稳定货币在未来利用跨链技术、身份体系与财政体系来推进发展。
最后，浩哥再次登场，为大家详细介绍了卡尔达诺的权益、挖矿与激励制度。这是大多数人非常关心的话题。 卡尔达诺的激励制度非常严谨，遵循让整个系统的权益达到某种分布形式，以适量的权益池为主体参与协议运，只要依靠个人的利益行事即为正确。 在爱好者交流环节，我们围绕了委托机制、代币发行、中国区未来规划等进行了有趣的讨论。
临走前，现场嘉宾、组织者、爱好者们共同合影留念，以纪念第一次的见面会圆满举办。
对了，还要感谢造就 TV 团队的大力支持，他们无私对本次活动进行了直播，让线上的爱好者们也能实时互动。
这场活动有惊无险地圆满结束了。
真感谢各位的大力支持，大家都是为了共同理念而努力。
梁宁老师说：这个世界如此精彩，我们当然不能置身事外。
很幸运，我们一直在努力，希望你也如此。

item.title: The AI x Crypto Revolution: Why 2025 Is Your Last Chance to Be Early
item.description: Everyone thinks AI will replace jobs. Everyone thinks crypto is just gambling. Nobody is talking about what happens when these two forces collide.
item.content:encoded:
## Introduction
Remember when everyone said the internet was just a fad? Or when crypto was dismissed as "magic internet money"?
Right now, two forces are colliding that will redefine what it means to be valuable in the modern economy—and almost nobody is paying attention to the intersection.
Everyone thinks AI will replace jobs. Everyone thinks crypto is just gambling. **Nobody is talking about what happens when these two forces collide.**
I'm going to show you the future that's already happening. Not theory. Not hype. Real examples from my own life that gave me back 10 hours per week and 100x my productivity.
If you're in crypto, you're already comfortable living on the bleeding edge. This is your next frontier.
## **The Great Shift: From "What You Know" to "How Fast You Execute"**
The rules of the game have fundamentally changed, and most people are still playing by the old rulebook.
**🌍 The Old World:** - What you know = Your value - Your degree = Your worth - Your title = Your identity
**🚀 The New World:** - How fast you execute = Your value - Your AI leverage = Your worth - Your output = Your identity
Here's the pattern that nobody's connecting: **Crypto decentralized finance. AI is decentralizing competence.**
Just as crypto removed the gatekeepers from money, AI is removing the gatekeepers from skill. You no longer need permission from institutions to become competent. You just need curiosity and the right tools.
## **My Wake-Up Call: The 4-Hour Project That Used to Take 4 Weeks**
Let me share a personal story that changed how I think about productivity forever.
Last month, I needed a custom crypto asset tracker. Nothing on the market fit my exact needs.
**The old me would have:** - Spent days learning to code from scratch - Paid a developer $2,000+ - Waited weeks for delivery - Gone through multiple revision rounds
**What I actually did:** - Described exactly what I wanted to Claude AI - Let it teach me the process step-by-step - Built a working prototype in 4 hours - Total cost: $20 in API credits
That's not a 2x improvement. That's not even 10x. **That's a 100x productivity gain.**
And here's the kicker: I now understand the code. I can modify it. I own the process. AI didn't replace my thinking—it amplified it.
## **The Content Creation Multiplier: How I Got 10 Hours Back Every Week**
Creating my weekly crypto content pillars used to consume half my week.
**The old workflow:** - 6 hours: Research across multiple sources - 3 hours: Writing and refining - 2 hours: Translating (I serve both English and Chinese communities) - 1 hour: Formatting for different platforms
**Total: 12 hours of grinding**
**The new AI-assisted workflow:** - 30 minutes: AI aggregates data from sources I specify - 1 hour: I add context, insight, and personal perspective - 7 minutes: AI translates and formats everything
**Total: Less than 2 hours**
**Where did those 10 hours go?**
I spend them with my family. I invest them in building new projects. I use them to think strategically instead of grinding tactically.
This isn't about working less—it's about having the freedom to choose where your energy goes.
## **The Pattern Everyone's Missing: AI = Leverage, Not Replacement**
Let's address the elephant in the room.
**People are scared:** "AI will take my job!"
**The reality:** "People using AI will take the job of people who don't."
We've seen this exact pattern before in crypto:
- **2017:** "Crypto will replace banks!" - **2025:** People with crypto knowledge replaced people without it
The technology didn't replace humans. **Humans with the technology replaced humans without it.**
It's not tech vs. humans. It's humans WITH tech vs. humans WITHOUT.
The same thing is happening right now with AI, except faster.
## **The Crypto x AI Connection: True Sovereignty**
Here's why this combination is so powerful:
**Crypto = Financial freedom** You don't need a bank's permission to transact.
**AI = Intellectual freedom** You don't need a boss's approval to create.
**Combined = True sovereignty** You don't need an institution's validation to succeed.
This is what "The Sovereign Individual" predicted back in 1997:
*"Individuals with laptop computers will be able to compete on equal terms with big organizations."*
**That future isn't coming. It's here. It's now. It's Tuesday.**
## **Real Examples of AI x Crypto Convergence (Happening Right Now)**
This isn't theoretical. Here's what's already live:
### **AI Trading Bots** - Analyzing on-chain data 24/7 - Executing trades based on patterns humans can't spot - Learning from every market movement
### **Smart Contract Auditing** - AI scanning code for vulnerabilities in seconds - Identifying scam patterns instantly - Protecting billions in assets
### **Sentiment Analysis** - Reading thousands of social signals simultaneously - Predicting market movements before they happen - Giving retail traders institutional-level insights
These tools aren't locked behind VC doors. They're available to anyone willing to learn.
## **My Personal AI x Crypto Stack (The Tools I Use Daily)**
Let me pull back the curtain and show you my exact setup:
### **🔍 Research:** - **Grok:** Real-time market sentiment and breaking news - **Claude:** Deep analysis and pattern recognition
### **🛠 Building:** - **Antigravity:** Full coding environment - **Claude Code:** Logic and debugging - **Gemini:** UI/UX design suggestions
### **⚡️ Productivity:** - **AI Translation:** Seamless Chinese ↔ English - **AI Calendar Agent:** Scheduling without the back-and-forth - **Email Agent:** First-pass responses and filtering
**Total cost: ~$100/month** **Value: Infinite leverage**
This is less than a fancy dinner, and it's given me superhuman capabilities.
## **The Uncomfortable Truth Nobody Wants to Hear**
Here it is, plain and simple:
**If you're not using AI daily by 2026, you're already behind.**
Not because AI is magic. Not because you're not smart enough.
**Because your competitors ARE using it.**
While you're spending 12 hours on a task, they're spending 2 hours and using the other 10 to:
📈 Ship 10x faster 📝 Create 10x more content 🧠 Learn 10x quicker 💡 Test 10x more ideas
The gap compounds daily.
### **History Repeats: Early Adopters Always Win**
- **Internet (1995):** "It's just a fad" - **Mobile (2007):** "Why would I need the internet on my phone?" - **Crypto (2017):** "It's tulip mania" - **AI (2023):** "It's just a tool"
In every case, the people who dismissed it got left behind. The people who went early built empires.
## **Your 4-Week Playbook: How to Start TODAY**
Stop overthinking. Stop waiting for the "perfect moment." Here's your action plan:
### **Week 1: Replace One Manual Task** - Start using ChatGPT for email drafts - Let AI summarize long articles or whitepapers - Use it to brainstorm project ideas
**Goal:** Get comfortable with AI assistance
### **Week 2: Automate One Workflow** - Set up portfolio alerts using AI tools - Create an automated research digest - Build a simple Discord/Telegram bot
**Goal:** See compound time savings
### **Week 3: Create One AI-Assisted Product** - Launch a weekly AI-curated newsletter - Build a simple tool that solves your own problem - Create educational content faster than ever
**Goal:** Shift from consumer to creator
### **Week 4: Build in Public** - Share your experiments on Twitter/X - Document your wins and failures - Connect with others on the same journey
**Goal:** Create accountability and opportunities
Don't overthink it. **Just start shipping.**
## **Why Crypto People Are Perfectly Positioned for This**
If you're reading this and you're already in crypto, you have an unfair advantage.
**You're already comfortable with:** - ✅ Bleeding edge technology - ✅ Complex systems that seem confusing at first - ✅ Taking calculated risks - ✅ Learning by doing - ✅ Being early to trends
You learned wallets, DEXs, gas optimization, and tokenomics—concepts that seemed impossible at first.
**You can absolutely learn prompts, agents, and AI workflows.**
Same energy. Different tool. Same massive upside.
## **The Sovereign Individual Thesis Is Playing Out in Real Time**
One person + AI + Crypto = Unstoppable.
You can now compete with: - VC-backed startups (alone) - Corporate teams with 50+ people (from your laptop) - Traditional institutions (without permission)
This isn't exaggeration. I'm doing it. Thousands of others are doing it.
**The question isn't whether this is possible. The question is: Will you be one of the people doing it?**
## **The Window Is Closing**
Here's the brutal truth: **In 2 years, this will be table stakes.**
Using AI won't be an advantage—it'll be expected. The opportunity isn't in using AI when everyone else is. **It's in using it NOW, while most people are still skeptical.**
The early adopter advantage is massive, but it's temporary.
Right now, you can: - Build an audience by documenting your AI journey - Create tools that solve problems before others notice them - Develop skills that will be in massive demand - Position yourself as an expert in the convergence
In 24 months, everyone will be doing this. The opportunity is in the next 24 days.
## **Your Call to Action: Choose Your Timeline**
You have three options:
**Option 1: Ignore this** Keep doing things the old way. Watch others lap you. Wonder what happened in 2 years.
**Option 2: Wait and see** Stay on the sidelines until it's "proven." Enter when it's crowded. Compete on hard mode.
**Option 3: Go early** Start today. Make mistakes. Learn fast. Build your unfair advantage while there's still space.
The people who went early on the internet built Google, Amazon, Facebook.
The people who went early on mobile built Instagram, Uber, WhatsApp.
The people who went early on crypto built... well, you know the stories.
**The people going early on AI x Crypto are building the next wave right now.**
---
## **Final Thought: This Is Your Moment**
The convergence of AI and crypto isn't coming—it's here.
Financial sovereignty + intellectual sovereignty = true freedom.
You don't need permission. You don't need credentials. You don't need a massive budget.
You just need curiosity, consistency, and the courage to start.

item.title: 云中遐想
item.description: 心心念念的呼伦贝尔之旅终于开始了。第一次乘坐飞机，云中穿梭间的遐想与感悟。
item.content:encoded:
**1**
心心念念的呼伦贝尔之旅终于开始了。第一次乘坐飞机的过程倒是十分顺畅，由于预订了早上 8 点的航班，提前一天到达了距离浦东国际机场很近的维也纳酒店，享受了一回贵宾的待遇以及酒店提供接送机服务。到了机场，熙熙攘攘的人群让机场更有生机，原来周末大家早就迫不及待地去玩了。
等待安检后，我们开始值机了，可是却在商务经济舱面前停步了，此时才发现我们预订的是头等舱，这真是意外之喜，虽说价格较高，我们也自得其乐。春秋航空的工作人员十分专业，熟练地将行李托运处理，并告知我们可前往 VIP 贵宾休息区享受。逛了半天，望着 2 号航站楼整齐划一的飞机与工作人员，脑中浮想联翩。
首次乘坐飞机，实在没什么能够炫耀的，心中更多是惊喜与期待。一生已过四分之一，这才算第一次体验到上天的感觉。我想，每个男生都有个梦想，划破苍穹，云中穿梭。小时候，总是很好奇地望着天空，那样地变幻莫测，时而晴空万里，时而云淡风轻。那时，真的觉得很神奇，**大自然总是会有很多我们无法理解的奥秘，直到现在，长大了，却更是发现自己的无知。**
登机后，我们耐心地等待起飞，飞机由慢到快，多次抬升高度，穿越对流层，感觉真的很奇妙。往窗外看，原来自己已经与云彩为伍，甚至超越了，肆意地俯瞰大地。说实话，一开始略显紧张，而后便深呼吸，事实证明我的担心是多余的。探出头去，发现我们已在平流层中，特别平稳，心情也格外舒畅。但空乘人员的专业以及整个行程的现场处理，让我感受到了乘坐飞机旅行的快乐。早在大家等待登机的时候，我特意拍了几张照片，展现了整个机场的全貌，也是对自己第一次乘坐春秋航空的经历，细想起来，真的很感激在过去这两年拼搏的我们。
整个人放松下来后，对着窗外的美景和流云，我不禁又想起了很多事情。
**2**
我以前一直幻想着那些空中飞人，看到别人这么频繁地穿梭在各个城市，眼中尽是羡慕，如此成就，已然算是成功了吧。
2009 年热映的《在云端》便是对频繁穿梭于城市之间的商务人士生活的诠释。这部电影由乔治克鲁尼主演，他饰演的是一名叫瑞恩 · 宾厄姆的公司裁员专家，飞来飞去为各地公司去解决麻烦，他的人生目标则是乘坐飞机积攒的英里数达到 1000 万，从而晋升为白金会员。
电影名称很美，一方面概括了空中奇遇，另一方面更是启迪思考：当瑞恩真的得到了白金卡的时候，突然间发现，这东西对于自己并没有太多的意义。**他面对着巨大的航班提示牌，望着花花绿绿数字的变更，仿佛每一处抖动都在叙述着自己的人生，冲上云霄却悬而未决的人生。** 瑞恩就是在这看似体面的生活下，不相信爱情，不相信婚姻，其实是难掩的寂寞与空虚，而由此为引导演用一明一暗的双线构思所一展的爱情故事，以及那最后不落俗套的结局，都让这部饱含温情的作品，为人一窥于生活的本质永远都会是爱与陪伴。
**看来每个人都有自己难念的经，他人的眼中光鲜靓丽，其实只是自己不甘放弃的无奈罢。**
**3**
工作也这么久了，我原先的一些想法或者态度也在慢慢转变了，现在真心主张做自己喜欢的事情，让自己在工作中快速撕裂成长，把工作和生活并联处理。**工作从来都不是一张工资条，我们所作出的贡献或是价值都是依靠自己的努力活出来的。** 在这方面我们只能自勉了。
此刻在飞机上的我，竟然有一种无法遏制的冲动，真心希望了解飞机的来龙去脉，一窥这承载现代生活的引擎是如何产生与发展的。我甚至想起了在《万物简史》是从普通人最常见的日常事物为大家展现细节以及历史趣事，给大家讲讲应该很有意思。
**4**
说起飞机的发明者，世界公认的就是莱特兄弟了。他们的传奇故事绝对值得一提。
1903 年 12 月 17 日，由美国奥维尔 · 莱特驾驶一架由兄弟俩自制的名叫「飞鸟」的飞机试飞成功，开辟了人类航空事业的新纪元。**首次试飞离开地面飞行 120 英尺（36.58米），持续飞行 12 秒，实现了持续的、有动力的、可操纵的载人飞行。** 莱特兄弟也因此成为得到世界各国承认的飞机发明人。
可是，任何创新在萌芽时都是被世俗所鄙视的，甚至那些当时的科学家们。二十世纪初，科学家们几乎都异口同声宣称，重于空气的飞行器是不可能造出来的，那些试图制造飞机的人都是白痴。
最先站出来反对飞机制造的，是最早用三角法测量月地距离的法国著名天文学家勒让德，他认为制造比空气重的飞行装置是异想天开。德国杰出的发明家西门子也发表了类似的见解。发现能量守恒原理的德国著名物理学家赫姆霍兹，则从物理学角度论证凭机器系统飞行是不可行的。美国著名天文学家西蒙 · 纽科姆通过大量的科学数据计算，证明比空气重的机械甚至根本不可能离开地面。
直到飞机发明的前夜，主流舆论还嗤之以鼻。1903 年 10 月 9 日《纽约时报》社论称：
> 一种翅膀发育不全的鸟，从开始飞腾直到能自由翱翔，如果说需要一千年，另一种动物，如果根本没有翅膀而不得不长出来，直到它能在天空飞翔，假如说需要一万年……那么，由数学家和机械师们联合制造的飞机，如果真要上天，大概需要一百万年到一千万年。
就在这篇社论发表后七天，莱特兄弟就试飞成功人类第一架飞机了。然而，主流社会对莱特兄弟的创新以及努力还是不予置评的。**每个年代的发明家是用生命在创新，听从想像力而不是权威和非议。所幸社会对他们是包容的。**
美国航空学家汤姆 · 克劳奇评述莱特兄弟的第一次飞行：
> 真正的影响力是在人类的想像上。1903 年以前，你会听到"如果上帝安排我们飞翔，他一定会给我们安上翅膀"；1903 年以后，人们说"如果人类能够造一架机器将我们带上天，那还有什么事情做不到？"
古今中外，**超越庸常的想像力是人类前进的动力。** 达 · 芬奇是第一个运用科学知识对飞行问题进行研究的人。在 1482 年至 1514 年间，他留下了许多对飞行的研究笔记和草图，并设计出了许多飞行器械，包括滑翔机、直升机和降落伞的雏形。
200 多年后，在开始了工业革命的英国，乔治 · 凯利爵士被公认为飞机的创造人和航空之父。他大约在 1801 年研究了鸟的推动力，并于 1804 年在旋转臂上试验了一架滑翔机。1810 年，他发表《论空中航行》，最早阐明了重于空气飞行器的基本飞行原理和飞机的结构布局，为后来的空气动力学奠定了基础。
后来，人类由航空时代迅速迈入航天时代，登上月球，把探测器送往火星和太阳系外……这一切需要更大胆的想像力。
毋庸置疑，**想象力是一切创新的源泉，当我们望着蔚蓝的天空与流云时，以梦为马，诗酒趁年华，亦是美事。**
不知不觉，飞机即将驶入满洲里的西郊国际机场了，呼伦贝尔的梦幻之旅同样在等待着我们。
希望你能够有所启发。

item.title: I Built a Working Tool in 4 Hours (And Nearly Destroyed It With These 4 Rookie Mistakes)
item.description: Your idea doesn't need to be revolutionary. It doesn't need to change the world. It just needs to work.
item.content:encoded:
## The Rush That Almost Ruined Everything
You know that feeling when an idea hits you at 2 AM and suddenly you can't sleep? That was me last Tuesday. I had this nagging problem—tracking Crypto ETFs was a mess of spreadsheets and browser tabs—and I finally decided: screw it, I'm building something TODAY.
Four hours later, I had a working tool. But here's the thing nobody tells you: those four hours included at least two hours of completely avoidable chaos that almost made me quit.
If you've been sitting on a side project idea, procrastinating because you think you need the "perfect approach," let me save you some pain. Here are the four mistakes that nearly killed my project before it could breathe.
## Mistake #1: I Coded First, Planned Never
**The Problem:** I dove straight into writing code because I was excited. Every five minutes, I'd think of a "critical feature" that suddenly became urgent. Want to add real-time alerts? Sure! How about historical comparisons? Why not! Social sharing? Absolutely essential!
**The Reality Check:** Half of those "urgent needs" were just dopamine-fueled excitement masquerading as product requirements.
**What I Should Have Done:** - Spent 15 minutes writing down the ONE problem I was solving - Listed the absolute minimum features needed to solve it - Saved the shiny add-ons for version 2.0
**The Lesson:** Your future self will thank you for those 15 minutes of planning. Random excitement is a terrible product manager.
## Mistake #2: I Made It Pretty Before I Made It Work
**The Problem:** I spent hours designing a gorgeous interface with perfect color schemes and smooth animations. Meanwhile, my backend was held together with prayer and console.log statements.
**The Brutal Truth:** Nobody cares how beautiful your button animations are if clicking them returns an error.
**What Actually Works:** 1. Get the data flowing first 2. Make the core functionality work (even if it's ugly) 3. Test with real data 4. THEN make it pretty
**The Lesson:** Data first, pretty stuff later. Always. A working prototype beats a beautiful wireframe every single time.
## Mistake #3: I Went Live Without Testing Locally
**The Big Oops:** I was so excited to see my tool "in the wild" that I started switching between Claude models mid-project, testing different configurations in production, and basically treating my live environment like a playground.
**The Chaos That Followed:** - Broken features that worked five minutes ago - Configurations that worked in one model but failed in another - No clear way to roll back when things went sideways
**The Smart Approach:** - Test everything locally first - Lock in your model and configuration - Only push to live when you're confident it works - Keep a backup of your last working version
**The Lesson:** Your live environment is not a laboratory. Test locally, deploy confidently.
## Mistake #4: I Thought I Was Too Smart for Documentation
**The Problem:** I saw the documentation for Antigravity and Claude Opus. I skimmed it. Then I thought, "I've done this kind of thing before, I can figure it out faster my own way."
Narrator: *He could not figure it out faster his own way.*
**The Humbling Truth:** Following the docs exactly sounds boring, but my "creative" shortcuts actually slowed me down. Every time I tried to be clever, I hit a wall and had to backtrack to do it the documented way anyway.
**What I Learned:** - Read the getting started guide completely - Follow the examples exactly as written first - THEN customize once you understand the patterns - Bookmark the API reference—you'll need it more than you think
**The Lesson:** Documentation exists because hundreds of people made the same mistakes before you. Learn from their pain, not your own.
## What Actually Worked: The Tech Stack That Saved Me
Once I stopped fighting myself, progress was surprisingly smooth:
- **Antigravity with Claude Opus** handled the heavy lifting - **Github MCP** for version control (which saved me when I broke things) - **Custom subdomains** to make it feel like a real product - **Focus on one problem:** Tracking Crypto ETFs without the spreadsheet nightmare
The tool works now. It's not perfect, but it solves my actual problem. And that's the whole point.
## The Real Lesson: Done Beats Perfect
Here's what nobody tells you about building things: you're going to mess up. You'll waste time on the wrong features. You'll break things that were working. You'll ignore advice and learn why it was advice in the first place.
But you'll also learn faster than any tutorial, course, or bootcamp could ever teach you.
**Building teaches you:**
- How to prioritize when everything feels important - Why best practices actually exist - How to debug your own thinking, not just your code - That "good enough and shipping" beats "perfect and planning"
## The Bottom Line
I turned a random idea into a working Crypto ETF tracker in four hours. I made mistakes that cost me time. But the tool exists now, and I learned more in those four hours than I did in the previous month of "thinking about building something."
Your idea doesn't need to be revolutionary. It doesn't need to change the world. It just needs to work.
So stop reading blog posts about building things (yes, including this one) and go build something.

item.title: 逆势黑马 Ethena 与空投复盘
item.description: 唯一真理：你的想法不重要，市场才是对的。
item.content:encoded:
前天 $ENA 上币，两天暴涨吸引眼球 📈
有幸参与官方第一季碎片活动：
- 2 月底存入 3 万 U，45 天共获得碎片 1300 万 - 目前领取 19,122 个 ENA，开盘卖出 1 万 U，币价最高 1.32 U
唯一真理：你的想法不重要，市场才是对的 ✅
## 一、如何发现
农历新年 BTC 领涨，我也适时调仓一些新币，体验其他赛道。
月底刷推特，发现万卉转发了关于 sUSDe 收益率与稳定性的一篇研究，当时觉得很新奇。
而后，BitWu 发推介绍了第一季碎片活动以及策略，我略加考虑，决定拿出余下的 3 万 U 换成 USDe，直接锁仓参与。
当然也考虑过脱锚风险，毕竟项目刚开始，还不知道后续市场接受程度。
当时讨论度一般，但增长很快，10 亿锁仓用了 1 个月时间。
## 二、参与逻辑
- 大佬站台，投资机构雄厚 - 对冲机制比 Restaking 要稳健，风险更低 - 巨鲸乐园，操作简单，无须撸毛，性价比高 - 保留现金流，尝试新赛道
## 三、上币思考
临近 4 月 1 日活动结束，3 月 23 日我先解锁了 USDe，担心后续币价波动。
期间上币与空投细则发币，3 月 26 日快照，独家刚回来琢磨后还是决定先持有 USDe，等待后续。
- 保持流动性，开盘后随时买卖，而非等待 7 天冷却 - 当初公告要求用户长期参与，深度绑定，USDe 持仓一直未动，等待上币
代币分配也是项目方控盘做局，ENA 暂未实质性赋能，能想到的就是帮助稳定 USDe 市值，一直滚雪球 ❄️
币安 Launchpad 也随即推出挖矿，一时间成为万众瞩目。
4 月 2 日空投分发，没想太多就全卖了。
期间有些插曲，至今还有 25% 代币未领取。
## 四、个人复盘
虽说卖飞永赚，今天梳理逻辑时发现了几个思考漏洞：
### 1、代币分配与解锁周期
这让我想起 Mavia，当时并未想清楚 ENA 持有逻辑，只是简单当成空投福利，牛市加持，大户线性解锁，前期都是市场在玩，自己却早早卖出。
### 2、市场叙事与做局
这套机制很有趣，但并非足够创新，加上了 ENA 这个治理代币想象空间就大了，相当于 Tether（USDT）与 Circle（USDC）发币，明显要做局吸引更大的资金。
50 亿锁仓是第二季的目标，现在已经 20 亿，等待有足够强力的稳定币市场份额。
这套体系牛市就能一直玩下去，发币时间节点与各种设计都很巧妙。
### 3、个人解锁
以为排名进入前 2000 名，有一半锁仓，卖出时就不假思索，然后重新锁仓 USDe，准备每月领工资。
今天发现已全部解锁，心情跌宕起伏。
ENA 市值差不多从 100 名开外直接上升到 70 名，市场对新事物的反馈火热，若是条件成熟，还有很大上升空间。
## 五、后续操作
只要好项目才会卖飞。
关键是你能否及时调整思维。
$SNEK / $BOME 都是最典型的案例。
昨晚暴涨时我重仓了 ENA，目前浮亏。
USDe 等待空投差额补回后全部卖出，不再参与第二季活动。

item.title: My Crypto Journey: From Buying the Top to Finding My Fortune
item.description: Focus on SNEK and things I love at this point. Just relieved I'm still here after 8 years. The Best Times Are Still Ahead of Us?
item.content:encoded:
## Introduction
Remember that person who bought Bitcoin at the absolute peak in 2017? Yeah, that was me.
While everyone around me was celebrating their gains, I was staring at a sea of red, wondering if I'd just made the biggest financial mistake of my life. Fast forward eight years, and I'm still here—battle-scarred, wiser, and somehow more passionate about this space than ever before.
This isn't a story about getting rich quick. It's about surviving the longest rollercoaster ride of my life, and why, despite everything, I wouldn't change a single thing.
## The Beginning: When Enthusiasm Met Reality
### Buying the Top (Because Of Course I Did)
2017 was electric. Everyone was talking about crypto. Bitcoin was mooning. Cardano was the "Ethereum killer." Every coin had a white paper that promised to revolutionize something.
I jumped in with both feet—right at the top.
BTC, ADA, and every hyped coin you can imagine filled my portfolio. Then came 2018, and I watched my investment shrink faster than my confidence. But here's the thing: instead of walking away like most people, I got curious. *What if this wasn't about quick gains? What if there was something bigger here?*
### The Metaverse Gamble: Quitting My Job
Most people thought I was crazy when I quit my job to dive into the metaverse. And honestly? They might have been right.
I spent countless nights building virtual businesses, competing with strangers from around the world. We were pioneers in a digital frontier that most people didn't understand. The project eventually died after two years—but something unexpected happened.
**Those strangers became some of my closest friends.** We're still in touch today, scattered across the globe, united by those wild early days when we believed we were building the future.
## The Education Phase: When I Found My Purpose
### Building Something Real
After the metaverse project collapsed, I could have given up. Instead, I pivoted.
For the next two years, I went all-in on something that actually mattered: - **Writing daily articles** to demystify crypto for newcomers - **Organizing meetups** where real faces replaced profile pictures - **Building communities** that supported each other through market swings
This wasn't about making money anymore. It was about creating something meaningful in a space that desperately needed authenticity.
Then the bear market hit like a freight train, and my portfolio sank to depths I didn't know were possible.
## Survival Mode: The Darkest Before Dawn
### March 2020: Crisis and Opportunity
The liquidity crisis in March 2020 should have been the end. Markets were bleeding. The world was shutting down. Fear was everywhere.
But that same crisis birthed the DeFi summer that pulled me back from the brink. 2021 was a whirlwind—the same manic highs and crushing lows of the previous cycle, but somehow I was better prepared.
During COVID lockdowns, while the world pressed pause, I kept exploring: - Stumbling through NFT projects - Learning DeFi protocols the hard way - Dipping into meme coins (yes, really)
**And you know what? The fun kept me going.** Not the money. The sheer, unpredictable adventure of it all.
## The Breakthrough: When Persistence Paid Off
### Finally Hitting the Jackpots
Since 2023, things shifted. SNEK, BOME, MNCHRMS, DOG—I finally hit several jackpots that multiplied my net worth.
But here's what nobody tells you about "winning" in crypto: the gains don't feel as dramatic as you'd imagine. After years of losses, comebacks, and near-misses, success feels more like relief than euphoria.
The landscape has changed too. Institutional adoption and ETFs have transformed crypto from the Wild West into something more... mainstream. It's different now, but different doesn't mean bad.
## The Biggest Lessons from 8 Years in the Trenches
### 1. **True Wealth Is the Network You Build**
More valuable than any coin in my portfolio are the connections I've made. The friends who answered my 2 AM questions about smart contracts. The community members who supported my content. The fellow believers who held through the darkness.
Your network is your real net worth.
### 2. **Never Exit, Even in the Darkest Times**
Every time I thought about quitting, I remembered why I started. The technology. The potential. The community.
**Staying in the game is 90% of the strategy.** You can't hit home runs if you're not at bat.
### 3. **Keep Your Keys, Keep Your Peace**
Not your keys, not your crypto. This old saying saved me more times than I can count. Exchanges fail. Platforms collapse. But your private keys? Those are yours forever.
Self-custody isn't just about security—it's about sovereignty.
### 4. **Follow Your Passion, Not Just Profits**
These days, I focus on SNEK and projects I genuinely love. The money flows easier when you're driven by authentic interest rather than FOMO.
### 5. **Embrace the Rollercoaster**
Crypto will test your emotional resilience like nothing else. The gains and losses are all part of the journey. Accept it. Embrace it. Learn from it.
## Moving Forward: Strategies for the Next Chapter
As I look ahead, here's what guides my decisions:
**🎯 Focus on Conviction Over Hype** - Invest in projects I understand and believe in - Ignore the noise on social media - Do my own research (always)
**🤝 Prioritize Community** - Surround myself with people who share my values - Contribute more than I take - Build relationships that transcend bull and bear markets
**💎 Practice Patience** - Stop chasing every new trend - Let quality investments compound over time - Remember that wealth is built in years, not days
**🧘 Protect My Mental Health** - Set boundaries with portfolio checking - Maintain interests outside crypto - Remember that my worth isn't tied to my net worth
## Conclusion: Still Here, Still Believing
Eight years ago, I bought the top and thought I'd made a terrible mistake. Today, I realize that "mistake" was the best thing that ever happened to me.
Not because I eventually made money (though that's nice), but because this journey taught me resilience, connected me with incredible people, and showed me that the greatest adventures often start with apparent failures.
**I'm just relieved I'm still here.** Still learning. Still building. Still believing in the technology that hooked me all those years ago.
The crypto space isn't perfect. It's volatile, unpredictable, and sometimes frustrating. But it's also innovative, inspiring, and full of possibilities that don't exist anywhere else.
Funny how crypto hooks you like that.
---
## Your Turn: Let's Talk
Have you been riding this rollercoaster too? Whether you're a 2017 veteran or a 2024 newcomer, I want to hear your story.
**Drop a comment below:** - How long have you been in crypto? - What's your biggest lesson learned? - What's your strategy moving forward?
Let's build this community together. After all, true wealth is the network we create.
And remember: **Never exit. Keep your keys. Keep your peace.**
*See you in the next cycle.* 🚀

item.title: Dreams from My Parents
item.description: This passage is about an epitomized conflict and reconciliation of a son and his parents.
item.content:encoded:
This passage is about an epitomized conflict and reconciliation of a son and his parents, and it is with reason to say that we are sharing the similar life scenarios.
**2**
She was an elite student all over when she was just a kid at school. Back then, living in the humble and cramped house, she was determined to crack all mystic puzzles without distractions from trivialities in life. Even equipped with a not-so-handsome bike, she was delighted to have her own and commuted back and forth. Our local school had been her home since she was thirteen. She was my alumna and a witness of our evolving small city.
Searching for knowledge and enrolling in a college were becoming unprecedentedly the golden opportunities for ordinary people since *National College Entrance Examination* was resumed in 1979.
The whole generation was inspired and devoted to the new market economy when only a selected few of students could be admitted to college with perfect job allocations. A lifelong secure job was all they aspired. Moreover, it would bring great glories and lift the whole family out of poverty through their own efforts.
Despite her mediocrity, she was daring to pursue her dream. Repeated recitations and mechanical memorization had went a long way in cramming up for politics, a mandated course for both literal arts and science. She persevered alone and became a college student after all.
In college, she would attentively learn food chemistry and processing, shunning much contact with the society. Landing a new world opened a unique chapter of life. Furthermore, the disparity between a city and small town astonished her and gave her enough incentive to climb a higher ladder, but unfortunately she failed.
**3**
At the same time, in a poor village more than 120 miles from her, another inspiring monologue unveiled. He was quite different from other peers in his hometown. All straight A's led him to contemplate beyond the regularities of life. By virtue of his unremitting efforts, he had become one of the best students and ultimately managed to attend a provincially renowned university in law. To his enduring pride, he had been the excellent role model in campus. After graduating from university, he respectively worked as official secretary, communist cadre and village leader.
Life could be more thrilling for him embark on until he and she had finally met. Afterwards, all I could remember was his title as a university professor and for more than two decades, I have been brought up under their wings.
Since migration, he had been preparing for the master degree of law in Wuhan University. With delicate balance of life and work, he contrived to become the first graduate student in our ancestry.
**4**
In a sense, it is fairly embarrassing to suggest that everything that I have achieved is dwarfed by their accolades in their twenties. More often than not, Dad lamented when we were strolling on campus at home:
> If only I had been more decisive and reflective in your decision-making moments.
The underlying message was all the more clear. **Cognitive differences are becoming predominant as we grope for further quest.** Whenever it comes to jobs, marriage, and life, we had disputes and uttered even heart-breaking words towards each other. In their eyes,
> It was frustrating that I failed to enroll in a high-ranking university and chose English much to my pure interest.
> It was disappointing that even though I finally passed the post-graduate exam, I could not land a superb job as everyone else did.
> It was even surprising that I decided to work with my roommates in a startup where my skills seemed irrelevant.
**However, as I reflected on every choice that I have made, maybe I was not all that competent and confident.** For quite a long time, I have declined any opportunity to listen with heart, thus depriving me of comprehensive insights behind everything they have done.
**5**
During the Spring Festival, when we quietly sat down and had sincere conversations, I somehow discovered their deep-rooted mentality. **Their memoir is extraordinarily spectacular and I would like to chronicle some major chapters and to share them with you.** After listening to their stories, I elaborated my thoughts and motives. Such social interaction was beyond my imagination in the past, so I could say that to some degree, the heart-warming talks dissipated our misunderstanding and relieved their doubts.
**To an outsider, I may be outstanding. To my parents, I may be stubborn and uncompromising.** Such sentimental paradox prevails in most cases.
> It is their hope that I will be self-reliant so as to survive in a metropolis.
> It is their hope that I will land a secure job so as to placate their minds.
> It is their hope that I will be goal-oriented in life so as to maximize my future potentials.
As for Mom, I used to violate almost every sentence that she dictated me. Besides, bombarded with unbearable chattering, I persisted regardless of her feelings. In my eyes, she is a meticulous, inarticulate and emotional person who has placed all of her unfulfilled dreams on me. **After serious conversations, it dawned on me that my words and actions have militated my relationships with her.** Deep in my heart, a sense of remorse always creeps in at the thought of my unobedience.
As for Dad, I hold great esteem for all he has accomplished and his responsibility for our family. **It is him who have done his best to make a better life, and endeavor to create superior advantages for my education and also my life.** He respects my decisions and I honor his. Fortunately, we have exchanged our ideas and opinions more and more later in my life.
**6**
My story may be unique, but it is convincing that through the transformation of my mentality, I am more mature and objective in communication with my parents and more likely to follow their hearts.
To conclude, I would like to list some of my humble strategies as follows:
> Embrace love in your life. Conflicts may occur, but love is what makes us complete in the world. Open your heart, sit down and discuss.
> Be responsible for your choices and actions. We are our own creators no matter what external advantages we may be endowed. Self-fulfilling prophecy, as you may have heard, will impinge on your life if you are convinced to create your beaten path.
> Be an independent thinker. The ability to perceive and reflect on your own thoughts are essential in objective analysis and personal values.
Good luck for everyone.
Hope we are all inspired.

item.title: 再见，美股投资
item.description: 历经波折，我总算把钱拿回来了。从美股定投到区块链的转折，以及跨境资金转移带来的思考。
item.content:encoded:
历经波折，我总算把钱拿回来了。这段故事还要从一年半前说起。
2017 年初，我开始投资美股，从选择券商到买卖标的，截止 2018 年 8 月也有 20 个月了。本来我的策略早就制定完毕，每两月定投一次，金额在 2 万元，投资标的就是大家熟知的 GAFATA 这六家世界互联网巨头。
起初倒也顺利，去年年底，我将自己一年的心得体会记录下来。我试图去寻找真正的机会，抓住那些趋利的不确定性，这两年来的投资经验与教训无一不在告诉我：**认知不够，想要实现财富增长的可能性几乎为零。** 如何合理对待他人的投资建议与想法，这才是考验一个人面对信息的主动分析和判断能力。
此外，我还详细地介绍了自己选择老虎证券作为券商的过程，虽然有诸多不便，还是毅然决然在另一个维度拥抱变化。
这是我当时的目标。
> 我希望抓住科技与互联网浪潮带来的机会，享受持续成长性的公司带来的长期增值，每一季度定期买入并长期持有 GAFATA 以及其他经过严谨分析筛选的美股，至少持有 10 年，这部分资金即便亏损也不会影响到个人生活，同时定期认真学习财务知识，定期更新投资报告与记录，做到有据可行，有理可依。
**1**
现在回想，很多地方差强人意。
第一就是国内政策变化，从 2018 年初开始，入金就变得异常困难，显然与国际形势关系密切，中美贸易战与国内经济形势双重叠加，国内资金想要出境，实在太困难了，况且这还是以证券投资为目的。过年回来，我就从未执行定投策略。
第二就是投资方向转移，以前我还会认真执行价值感知训练，每月定期复盘与整理数据，大致了解这六家公司的业务表现，现在却不太关注，除了大跌会让心态失衡外，其他时候基本上属于佛系状态，更多地开始了在区块链世界的学习与理解。
时过境迁，每个人都逃不过周期，今年大家能明显感受到凛冬将至的氛围，和前几年的保增长有所不同，从金融角度出发，无论是股权投资、基金、P2P、股票、外汇等领域遭遇了巨大危机，当然也包括区块链市场。
我的几位朋友发出了类似的观点，既有实体经济，也有金融机构，总结下来，其实就是，保存实力活下来。此时大家的对策都变得更为保守，无论是企业还是个人，都要想尽办法活下来，未来才有希望。
鉴于以前种种，我重新梳理了自己的资产配置，显然把资金拿回来要更为关键。**有句话不是说，现金为王。** 现在就是这个时点。
**2**
于是乎，我开始研究如何把落袋为安。
其实，我心里也没底，当初入金时其实这一环并没有测试过，现在想要拿钱回来担心限制太多。事实上，这些都是一个人心里作怪，毕竟那么多钱自己无法掌控。
我先把几只股票都卖了，当时说是要持有 10 年，却现在就跑了。人的心理还真是奇怪啊。
出金过程中，我碰到的心理斗争还真不少：
一是中转行填写，按照出金流程，登录盈透证券填写收款银行时，碰见了很多不了解的信息。第一反应是特别麻烦，此后去查询了并且拨打电话，心里也长舒一口气。
二是单笔跨境汇款数额过大可能会被退回，论坛中有很多朋友都担心这个问题，加上中国外汇局监管，一般来说，超过 3000 美金都要审核。
其实我也很焦虑，早知道这么复杂，我还不如放在余额宝里。不过转念一想，或许还有希望呢，第一次去银行，说是朋友汇入，要买房使用，其余一概不知，假装理直气壮，工作人员盘问再三，说是上报外汇局，如果通过会再回复。第二天竟然告诉我通过了，再来一趟即可，终于把大部分资金都转回来了，加上近期人民币贬值，也算多赚了一笔。
当天从建行出来，一方面心里窃喜，转账竟然能够如此顺利，另一方面也在思索，未来社会究竟会怎样？显然，这样的中心化信任机构完全无法满足我的个人需求。
几个问题一直在脑子里打转：
**一、为什么国家要严格控制资金进出？**
**二、为什么个人无法自由投资全球证券市场？**
**三、若是用数字货币来改变会不会更简单？**
**3**
**大多数时候，我们都容易被习惯所裹挟，生活如何、现实世界如何，我们很少会好奇当初为什么如此设计，因而就会对很多现象或是反常习以为常。**
上面的前两个问题本质就是，国家实力的体现，这一方面在中国尚未建立完善的金融体系与国家硬实力的情况下，还是依然存在。
最常见的理论就是蒙代尔不可能三角。目前中国的选择是放弃资本自由流动，中国需要汇率稳定，央行无法通过市场手段实现汇率固定和资本自由流通，所以资本管制就成为了必须。
换个角度，其实货币本质上是国家信用。世界大国比拼的无非就是国家信用，美元一直以为来作为投资标的之一，还是依靠背后美国强大实力和国家信用。
**4**
我更关心的是第三个问题，因为区块链和比特币这两个字眼自 2017 年变得异常火爆。
信息革命带来了互联网技术，现在人人都享受这一技术带来的巨大红利中，而区块链技术则是让我们从信息到价值转移的过程。
**区块链是全球统一、分布式、公开的数据库，特点之一就是不可篡改。**
**比特币则是区块链技术的应用之一，为了满足去中心化的点对点交易，让参与者透明可信，同时拒绝作恶。**
